title: Four-Variable Karnaugh Map Structure
description: A visual representation of a Karnaugh map used for simplifying Boolean algebra expressions with four input variables (A, B, C, D).
relationships: The grid is labeled with Gray code for rows (AB) and columns (CD) to ensure only one variable changes between adjacent cells
Each cell represents a unique combination of the four input variables and contains a value (1, 0, or don't care)
Adjacent groupings of '1's (including wrap-around edges) are identified to form product terms for the simplified Boolean expression
